label: 189_hr_estimates enacting_medicare_direct spending
label_human: Medicare device coverage and opioid bills
description: CBO estimates on Medicare's automatic coverage of FDA-approved devices for the opioid epidemic. Bills aimed at addressing the crisis through healthcare spending and regulatory changes.

earliest_articles.title: H.R. 452, the Medicare Decisions Accountability Act of 2011
earliest_articles.excerpt: As ordered reported by the House Committee on Ways and Means on March 8, 2012 H.R. 452 would repeal the provisions of the Affordable Care Act (ACA) that established the Independent Payment Advisory Board (IPAB) and created a process by which that Board (or the Secretary of the Department of Health and Human Services)…

earliest_articles.title: S. 3391, Taking Essential Steps to Testing Act
earliest_articles.excerpt: As ordered reported by the Senate Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions on September 19, 2012 S. 3391 would amend the Public Health Service Act to revise sanctions that the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) must impose on clinical laboratories under certain circumstances. newest_articles.title: S. 1098, Opioid Overdose Data Collection Enhancement Act
newest_articles.excerpt: S. 1098 would amend the Comprehensive Opioid, Stimulant, and Substance Use Program (COSSUP) to allow the Department of Justice (DOJ) to award grants to state, local, and tribal governments to track drug overdoses. The bill also would allow coalitions of law enforcement agencies to receive funds for this purpose and…

newest_articles.title: H.R. 7931, Preserving Emergency Access in Key Sites Act
newest_articles.excerpt: H.R. 7931, the Preserving Emergency Access in Key Sites Act, would increase Medicare payments for certain rural ambulance suppliers and hospitals. Payments would rise to 101 percent of what is termed reasonable cost, which is greater than amounts under current law. CBO estimates that enacting the bill would increase…

central_articles.excerpt: As ordered reported by the House Committee on Ways and Means on May 16, 2018 Summary On May 16, 2018, the House Committee on Ways and Means ordered seven bills to be reported related to the nation’s response to the opioid epidemic. central_articles.title: H.R. 1691, Ensuring Patient Access to Critical Breakthrough Products Act of 2023
central_articles.excerpt: As ordered reported by the House Committee on Ways and Means on June 27, 2024 On This Page Bill Summary H.R. 1691 would require Medicare to automatically cover devices approved under the Breakthrough Devices Program of the Food and Drug Administration (FDA).
